name: Capellades, La Torre de Claramunt, and Vilanova d'Espoia Loop
name_original: Montes de Vila-Seca — circular por Sant Salvador de Vilanova d'Espoia
description: This moderate 7.5-mile (12.0 km) jogging route in Anoia climbs 883 feet (269 metres) and passes historic landmarks.
region_intro: Jogging around Capellades offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and agricultural areas. The region features varied terrain, from the Anoia river basin to the higher elevations around La Pobla de Claramunt. Runners can expect paths that traverse open fields, pass through small villages, and occasionally climb moderate ascents. The area provides a mix of natural and semi-urban environments suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Capellades The most popular jogging route is PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route offers a challenging run through the varied terrain of the Conca d'Òdena. Another top favourite among local runners is Tres Mollons Circular Trail from La Pobla de Claramunt, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail provides scenic views as it circles the Tres Mollons hills. Local runners also love the Capellades, La Torre de Claramunt, and Vilanova d'Espoia Loop, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through multiple towns and rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Capellades is defined by river paths, rolling hills, and routes connecting local villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier runs along the Anoia river to more demanding trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Capellades are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,600 runners have used komoot to explore Capellades's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Capellades?
A: Capellades offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 130 routes available. These range from easier paths to more challenging trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Capellades?
A: The running routes in Capellades are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,600 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy running paths suitable for beginners in Capellades?
A: Yes, Capellades has several easy running paths perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed pace. There are 9 routes specifically classified as easy, often following river paths or flatter terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Capellades?
A: Many of the running routes in Capellades are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1112884413/tres-mollons-circular-trail-from-la-pobla-de-claramunt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mollons Circular Trail from La Pobla de Claramunt&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views as it loops around the hills.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Capellades?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most outdoor trails in the Capellades area. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

Q: Are there any interesting natural sights or landmarks to see while running in Capellades?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in natural beauty. You can discover highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Deus Fountains&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2155690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Sala&lt;/a&gt;, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3122128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capellades Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique experience, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prehistoric Park of Capellades&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites along the running routes?
A: Yes, the Capellades area is dotted with historical sites. Runners can encounter impressive structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3270107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Claramunt Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Quintí Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past amidst your run.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Capellades?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions in Capellades, with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making year-round running possible, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Is parking available near the jogging routes in Capellades?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Capellades and near the starting points of many trails, especially in or around the villages. Look for designated parking areas or street parking where permitted.

Q: How can I get to the running routes in Capellades using public transport?
A: Capellades is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger towns. You can often reach the town center, which serves as a starting point for many routes. For specific route access, it's best to check local bus schedules and plan your run accordingly.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Capellades?
A: Many of the easier and moderate routes in Capellades are suitable for families, especially those that follow river paths or have less significant elevation changes. These trails offer a great way to enjoy the outdoors together, often passing through pleasant landscapes.

Q: Where can I find a cafe or refreshments near the running trails?
A: Capellades and the surrounding villages like La Pobla de Claramunt offer various cafes and establishments where you can refuel or grab a drink. Many routes pass through or near these villages, making it convenient to find refreshments before, during, or after your run.
tips: Beautiful castle located in the center of the town of La Torre de Claramunt, a Gothic Romanesque castle, dating from the 11th century, well preserved and with a long and beautiful history, if you are on a route through the area, it is worth spending some time to your visit.
In the middle of the town of Vilanova d'Espoia we find the church of San Salvador, which together with its surroundings makes it a beautiful place to pass by and enjoy the area as a whole.
River that joins the Torrent del Manou and the Torrent d'Oriol and forms the Riu d'Agost that descends in the direction of Anoia and towards the Mediterranean Sea.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Vila-Seca circular route is rated as moderate for jogging. While it's not excessively long at approximately 12 km, it does involve about 270 meters of elevation gain. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should be able to complete it, but be prepared for some uphill sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'Montes de Vila-Seca' in the name, you can expect varied terrain. While the general Vila-seca area has many flat paths, the 'Montes' suggest some hilly or elevated sections. This could include unpaved paths, gravel, and inclines, similar to some easier mountain biking routes in the region. Be prepared for surfaces that are not always smooth pavement.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Montes de Vila-Seca circular route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a mix of natural and historical sights. You'll pass by the historic Castle of La Torre de Claramunt, the charming Church of Vilanova d'Espoia, and the natural beauty of the Riu d'Agost. The scenery will likely shift from more natural, possibly wooded or elevated views, to local landmarks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route by Sant Salvador de Vilanova d'Espoia, it's generally best to look for parking within Vilanova d'Espoia itself or a nearby accessible point. Specific parking areas for this exact route are not widely documented, but local village parking or designated spots near the starting point would be your best bet. Always check local signage for restrictions.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Ensure you carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for jogging in Anoia?
faqs.answer: For general jogging routes like the Montes de Vila-Seca circular, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required. Most natural areas and trails in the Anoia region are freely accessible to the public. However, always be respectful of private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The region generally experiences pleasant temperatures and abundant sunshine throughout most of the year, making it favorable for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for jogging. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon start is recommended if you visit during those months.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of El Camí IT6TR6 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Camí de Carme, Camí d'Espoia, Camí de Cal Muntades, and local streets like Carrer de Pau Casals and Carrer Costa de les Creus.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate jogging route with varied terrain, wear comfortable athletic clothing suitable for the weather. Sturdy running shoes with good grip are recommended for potentially uneven surfaces. Bring enough water, especially on warmer days, and consider snacks for energy. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Vilanova d'Espoia, being a local village, will likely have some basic amenities like a local bar or small shop. For a wider selection of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, you might need to venture to larger nearby towns. It's a good idea to check availability in advance, especially if you plan to stay overnight or dine out.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: As indicated by its name, 'Montes de Vila-Seca — circular by Sant Salvador de Vilanova d'Espoia', this is a loop trail. You will start and finish at roughly the same point, making it convenient for parking and exploration.
